config.bannerHtml: One genuine RED: COGS. 23.5% April, 21.4% May vs a 19% budget (consumption variance −₹8.8L in April alone) — War Room active. April closed at an EBITDA loss of −₹22L on year-opening one-offs; May recovered to +₹40L (+15%) and beat budget. Everything else on the scoreboard is Green or Amber; the marketing Amber is by design through August.
config.honestRead: Three things are simultaneously true going into FY27, and the plan honours all three: (1) the business has scale (₹23.1 Cr FY26 revenue) but not yet sustained profitability (₹0.53 Cr cleaned EBITDA, and April FY27 was an outright loss); (2) FY27 demands a step-change to ₹13.17 Cr — explicitly the optimistic case, conditional on COGS returning to 19% and ODC doubling to ₹10 Cr; (3) four new outlets land on top of all of that, funded by a family-office bridge that peaks dangerously in September and only clears if Q4 delivers.

kpis.note: April was a loss (year-opening one-offs); May +15% margin, beat budget by ₹12.3L
Apr 23.5% · May 21.4% vs 19% budget · every 1pt = ₹40L · ACTIVE CRISIS
beat budget by ₹43.5L · Apr ₹2.02 Cr (on budget)
unsecured family funding already on the books — the bridge is deep in

riskFlags.t: COGS is the one genuine RED. 23.5% April, 21.4% May against a 19% target. Every 1pt = ₹40L of EBITDA. The ₹13.17 Cr anchor is conditional on closing this. COGS War Room (Rock 8) is active — weekly meeting, per-outlet yield audits, supplier renegotiation calendar live.
Marketing spend above 3% through August — by design. Agency + in-house run in parallel during the handover (HoM hired). A planned transition cost, not an overrun. Normalises to ~2.7% from September.
September is the peak-crunch month. Gandhinagar opening + Mumbai pre-opening/opening + Beshak signing now all cluster here, in the weakest season. Peak likely exceeds ₹7.24 Cr — re-model required.

Key-person concentration. SS and AJ each own 4–5 critical functions plus most rocks, and the exec team is green by our own admission. Tech lead now named; SS still to deputise one of {bridge, new-outlet dev}.
May recovery is real. April's −₹22L EBITDA loss became +₹40.4L (+15% margin) in May, beating budget. ODC Sales Head hired; 50% advance now mandatory on bookings > ₹50,000.

scoreNote.9: Above 3% through August by design (agency + in-house parallel). Annotated as a planned transition cost; normalises from September.
scoreNote.10: New metric — live from Q2. Staff retention is now a scoreboard metric, not a Q1 to-do.

rocks: Org mapped. Zero unresolved role overlaps. Q1-critical hires made. Staff retention metric live.
Brand identity, marketing plan, content cadence locked. Revenue is not the target.
SOP rulebook, T&C, vendor policy, billing, season/offseason pricing, brochure, client database.
Inventory control + KPI dashboard scoped. Dashboard live Q2.
Every leadership owner reads their P&L lines by the 10th of each month and explains variances. No exception.
HoM hired. In-house team build underway; agency off-boarded by September.
(a) 100% PO compliance via Petpooja. (b) Structured petty cash. (c) Zoho maker/checker. Tracked separately.
Weekly COGS meeting running. Per-outlet yield audits done. Supplier renegotiation calendar with named dates live. Weekly COGS dashboard active.

steps: Largest single-month draw — pre-FY27 pending capex + Mumbai signing. (Gandhinagar build now shifts later with its September opening.)
Gandhinagar opening + Mumbai pre-opening & opening + Beshak signing now all cluster here. Highest bridge-risk month, in the weakest season — peak likely exceeds ₹7.24 Cr.
Q4 portfolio strength (₹250L+ EBITDA/month Jan–Mar) repays the bridge.
FY27 closes net cash-positive — in the 19% COGS case.
tripwires: Emergency cash review within 48 hours. Defer non-critical capex. SS + CFO + family-office contact.
Full-day leadership reset. Bridge timeline revision. Beshak Dec capex decision reviewed.
Collections escalation. New bookings require full prepay until AR < 30 days.
Escalate the War Room. Pricing + sourcing levers pulled harder; review against the 23% scenario.
COMBINED: COGS ~22% AND Mumbai < 60% at M3 — NEW
Treat as one trigger — plausibly pushes the bridge past ₹8.5 Cr. SS + CFO + family office, freeze Beshak capex, model the combined peak.
bridge.cashNote: Two cash fragilities to name: (1) Repayment is a single-quarter bullet — the whole bridge clears on Jan–Mar throwing off ₹250L+/month, with no buffer if Q4 disappoints by even 10%. (2) The ₹10 Cr facility is a single-provider family-office line — confirm it is documented & committed, not a handshake, before the September peak, and pre-arrange a fallback working-capital line. Reality from the books (BS 15 Jun): ₹25.4 Cr of unsecured parent loans already sit on the balance sheet (Siddhant ₹22.8 Cr, Mitaben ₹1.6 Cr, Vidhiben ₹1.0 Cr) — the bridge is not a future facility, it is already substantially drawn. Beshak villa WIP is ₹1.95 Cr spent of the ₹5.09 Cr commitment. Confirm how much of the ₹10 Cr FY27 standby remains as undrawn headroom going into September.

risks: 1. COGS doesn't migrate to 19%
Every 1pt = ₹40L. 23% case = ₹11.2 Cr EBITDA, +₹0.9 Cr cash.
ALREADY TRIGGERED — Apr 23.5%, May 21.4%.
COGS War Room active (Rock 8): weekly meeting, per-outlet yield audits, supplier renegotiation calendar.
red
2. Mumbai underperforms ramp
The swing factor — mature contribution exceeds Jaipur + Gandhinagar + Beshak combined.
Pre-opening reservation < 40% Surat launch-week. Month 3 < 60% of Surat month-3.
₹10–15L paid push pre-approved (flagged as a floor — likely under-sized). Month 3: full-day reset. Beshak Dec capex revisited.
amber
3. ODC plateaus at ₹8 Cr
₹1.5 Cr revenue miss.
Q2 pipeline < ₹7 Cr annualised.
ODC Sales Head hire accelerated. VV reviews pipeline monthly. Catering rebuild reviewed.
amber
4. Headcount growth breaks quality
Brand promise breaks. Hardest to recover late.
Mystery diner < 7.5/10 at 2+ outlets same month.
HK triggers full outlet audit. Manager + SOP retraining within 2 weeks. No new opening until cleared.
amber
5. Beshak underperforms — capital committed pre-proof
₹1.5 Cr FY27 miss. FY28 loses ₹4–5 Cr. ₹5.09 Cr already committed (₹1.95 Cr spent).
Month 2 < 50% of Capiche Piplod launch trajectory.
Jan–Feb re-positioning sprint. FY28 scale decision deferred to Apr 2027. FLAG: stage capex behind a cheap concept test rather than commit-then-pivot.
amber
6. Correlated / combined stress — NEW
2–3 tripwires firing together in one soft quarter — the real failure mode.
COGS ~22% AND Mumbai < 60% at Month 3 in the same window.
Treat as a single trigger — bridge plausibly > ₹8.5 Cr. SS + CFO + family office, freeze Beshak capex, model combined peak.
red
7. Bridge provider / single-line risk — NEW
Facility pulled/repriced at Sep peak = existential.
Facility undocumented, or parent liquidity signal at peak.
Confirm ₹10 Cr is papered & committed now. Pre-arrange a fallback working-capital line as backstop.
red
8. Key-person concentration — NEW
Two people are the bottleneck; the model assumes they don't hit their limit.
2+ rocks owned by the same person slip in the same month.
Reassign the DRI, don't coach. Tech lead now named; SS still deputises one of {bridge, new-outlet dev}.
amber
folio: Scale aggressively. Replicable via central kitchen.
Defend, don't expand in FY27. Define what Aiko owns.
Test the concept. FY28 tells us if it scales.
Double from FY26. Full rebuild. 50% advance mandatory.
Brand-building year. PMF before revenue scale.
rhythm: Outlet teams + a corporate stand-up (SS/AJ + rock DRIs)
Yesterday's sales & COGS/wastage number, today's plan, one blocker each — "where are you stuck?" During the War Room, the daily COGS + wastage check lives here.
Rock progress vs done-when, every scoreboard Red, and the week's single priority. The weekly COGS War Room meeting feeds straight into this.
The P&L habit (Rock 5): every owner reads and explains their own P&L lines and variances — straight off the Budget Variance tab. Full 11-metric scoreboard review.
Reset the 8 rocks, set next quarter's Critical Number + theme, re-model the bridge, and celebrate/reward the quarter's theme win.
Rebuild Book One, review/reset the BHAG, set the formal FY28 plan (April 2027, once Mumbai Q3 data is in), and run the people / Topgrading review.
